steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The dash before the blank signals that what follows will clarify or restate the preceding technical phrase. The blank introduces a definition of "cognate replacement" — the passage needs a transition that signals 'here is what that term means.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'likewise,': signals similarity between two separate ideas. The definition that follows is not a parallel idea; it restates the same idea. ✗
  • Choice B — 'in addition,': signals an additive new point. Defining a term already mentioned is not adding a new point. ✗
  • Choice C — 'that is,': the standard phrase for restating or defining something just named. Perfectly introduces the definition of "cognate replacement." ✓
  • Choice D — 'for example,': introduces a specific instance of a general claim. The definition is not an example; it is a restatement. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
